blockers.description: The prompt asks for cards with natural names, but it is unclear what exact search terms should be used to accomplish this.
example_questions: Which type of names should I search for when looking for cards with natural names?
What names would be natural names?
What type of names would you consider as "natural"?
blockers.resolution: The cards with natural names are those that contain the "element" word. Strictly name LIKE "%element%".

blockers.description: The prompt requires comparing with the most played MTG monster. This information is not present in the dataset and needs to be found online.
example_questions: What is the most played MTG card?
What card should I consider as the most played MTG card?
Could you provide the name of the most played MTG card?
blockers.resolution: The most played MTG monster currently are those named "Lurrus of the Dream-Den" (there are multiple versions).

blockers.description: The "average balanced power" is used in the prompt and is mentioned in the business information as a formula that takes into consideration a card's power (only for numeric values) and rarity, giving a higher value to rare cards, but the exact formula is not specified.
example_questions: What exact formula should I use when calculating the average balanced power?
How can I calculate the average balanced power?
Which is the formula I should use when calculating the average balanced power?
blockers.resolution: The average balanced power is calculated as the average of power*rarity, where "rarity" is worth 1 for uncommon and common cards, and 2 for rare and mythic cards.

blockers.description: The cards table has columns named info*, which makes it impossible to know wich one mentions if a card is foiled or not
example_questions: How can I know if a card is foiled?
How should I know if a card is foiled?
Which column can I use to know if a card is foiled?
blockers.resolution: The column info6 from the cards table is the definitive column for determining whether a card is foiled or not. It has a value of 1 if foiled and 0 if not foiled.
